channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci and Milley in an effort to guard against potential 'revenge' by Trump
item.description: The decision by Biden comes after Donald Trump warned of an enemies list filled with those who have crossed him politically or sought to hold him accountable for his attempt to overturn his 2020 election loss and his role in the storming of the U.S. Capitol on Jan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump to deploy military to border, end Biden parole policies in flurry of Day One executive orders
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday will sign a slew of border-related executive orders, sending troops to the border and also ending Biden-era parole policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inauguration Day Latest: Trump kicks off Inauguration Day pageantry with morning church service
item.description: Donald Trump, who overcame impeachments, criminal indictments and a pair of assassination attempts to win another term in the White House, is set to be sworn in as the 47th U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Do US adults support Trump's agenda? Here's what an AP-NORC poll shows
item.description: A poll finds that despite Donald Trump's claims of an “unprecedented and powerful mandate,” the incoming Republican president lacks broad support for some of his top priorities.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Thomson-Deveaux And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Law enforcement on high alert for Trump inauguration after 2024 assassination attempts
item.description: Federal, state, and local law enforcement officials will be on high alert on Jan. 20 when President-elect Donald Trump gets sworn into office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmaker pushes bill to rename Nashville International Airport after Donald Trump
item.description: Tennessee Republican State Rep. Todd Warner wants to rename Nashville International Airport to Trump International Airport. If this bill is passed, it will take effect on Jan. 1, 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How technology has changed inauguration coverage
item.description: Donald Trump's inauguration can be livestreamed via the internet in real time all over the world. It wasn't always so easy to tune into a presidential address.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden urges Americans to 'keep the faith' as he spends final full day as president in South Carolina
item.description: NORTH CHARLESTON, S.C. (AP) — Joe Biden spent his final full day as president Sunday in South Carolina, urging Americans to “keep the faith in a better day to come” and reflecting on the influence of both the civil rights movement and the state itself in his political trajectory.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump lays wreath at Tomb of the Unknown Soldier ahead of inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump participated in a wreath-laying ceremony at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier on Sunday, the eve of his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's influence spreads through state capitols in both substance and symbolism
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A new year of lawmaking and politicking is underway in U.S. statehouses. Governors are acting quickly to outline their agendas. And in many cases, the influence of President-elect Donald Trump already is rippling through states in both symbolic and substantive ways.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1985 was the last time an inauguration was indoors. Ronald and Nancy Reagan felt they had no choice
item.description: Ronald and Nancy Reagan were disappointed. That's what White House press secretary Larry Speakes told reporters on Jan. 18, 1985, after the Republican president and first lady decided to hold his second inauguration indoors because of an unusually cold weather forecast.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Merrick Garland exits with his record under scrutiny and the Justice Department bracing for upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — During hearings on Merrick Garland's nomination to be President Joe Biden's attorney general, the longtime federal appeals court judge told senators in 2021 that he hoped to “turn down the volume” on the public discourse about the Justice Department and return to the days when the
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The inauguration is moving indoors. Here's what to know about the changed plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sub-freezing temperatures forecast for Monday in Washington are prompting President-elect Donald Trump to take the oath of office from inside the Capitol Rotunda. Almost everyone visiting Washington won’t be able to see it in person.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Mel Gibson 'surprised' to be named Hollywood envoy
item.description: Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Durbin seeks information from Patel personnel files at national security agencies
item.description: Editor's note: This story has been updated to correct the committee that Durbin leads. Sen. Dick Durbin (Ill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Renew your driver’s license from a browser? New Walz budget seeks online option
item.description: In his newly proposed budget, Gov. Tim Walz suggests allowing online renewal for driver’s licenses. Walz said the move would increase filing fee revenue, while making it more convenient for drivers.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: What’s next for Kamala Harris
item.description: Inauguration Day marks the first time in more than 20 years that Kamala Harris will not be in public office. “It is not my nature to go quietly into the night,” she told allies on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams to meet President-elect Trump in Florida meeting
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams will be meeting with President-elect Donald Trump on Friday, a spokesperson for the mayor has confirmed. Adams is traveling to Palm Beach, Florida, to discuss the New Yorker’s priorities.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, PIX 11

item.title: Court aims for fall trial date in sexual misconduct lawsuit against Fletcher
item.description: The lawsuit facing Nathan Fletcher over allegations of sexual misconduct could move to a civil trial as soon as fall 2025, as both sides continue to bump heads over text messages between the ex-supervisor's accuser and a friend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Ellison joins 13 states to defend health care coverage for DACA recipients
item.description: The effort is in response to a legal challenge led by Kansas and several other states against a Biden administration rule.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Scott Stringer announces run for New York City mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former New York City Comptroller Scott Stringer is joining the 2025 race for New York City mayor, he announced on PIX11 News on Thursday. He has a long political career, previously serving as the Manhattan borough president and a New York State Assembly member.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's pick for secretary of the Interior Department is Doug Burgum. Here's what to know
item.description: Doug Burgum, former North Dakota governor and businessman is President Donald Trump's pick to be Secretary of the Interior. The confirmation hearing is scheduled for Thurs., Jan 16, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota courts may get final say in government's constitutional clash
item.description: An ugly opening to Minnesota’s legislative session and a campaign season that won’t end is fueling lawsuit after lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Local reactions mixed as Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal moves forward
item.description: The Middle East and the world are celebrating the Israel-Hamas ceasefire as a possible step toward peace, but many are still cautious about the deal.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok ban to impact local businesses, content creators
item.description: San Diego is home to talented and creative content creators who make a living on TikTok. While many are able to make a full-time income on the app, some are also helping small businesses in return, who say the app has inadvertently helped them tremendously.
item.dc:creator: Zara Barker, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. Murphy calling for cellphone ban in NJ classrooms
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (PIX11) - Could there be a day when there will be no cellphones in New Jersey classrooms? In his State of the State address on Tuesday, Gov. Phil Murphy announced his support for legislation that would direct school districts to adopt policies that ban cellphones in K-12 classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: NYC mayor: More beds for mentally ill people, more cops on subways
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- With more NYPD officers heading into the subway system during the overnight hours, New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he is also looking to get mentally ill homeless people off the trains with a new option.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: End to daylight saving time among bills for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: A Republican lawmaker who represents parts of six rural Nevada counties is sponsoring a bill to take the state off daylight saving time — one of the 200 bills written so far.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge clears the way for release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department can publicly release its investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case, a federal judge said Monday — the latest ruling in a court dispute over the highly anticipated document days before Trump is set to take office again.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
